video:video.video:title: Sirens sound to warn of an incoming missile in central Israel
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded to warn of an incoming missile in Central Israel on Wednesday. An AP reporter heard loud booms in Tel Aviv as the windows of buildings shook from reverberations.

video:video.video:description: Airstrikes continued to hit Tehran followed by plumes of smoke rising above buildings in Iran's capital.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and the U.S. military say a U.S. blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas will begin on Monday.

video:video.video:description: Raja Ampat, a remote archipelago in eastern Indonesia, has been long regarded as a model for ocean conservation.

video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he would not apologize for sharply criticizing Pope Leo saying that the pontiff “went public” in his criticisms of the war in Iran.

video:video.video:description: Washington Capitals captain and NHL all-time goals leader Alexander Ovechkin discusses his future plans as he considers retirement after 21 seasons.

video:video.video:description: LaMelo Ball apologized for his role in the injury to Bam Adebayo in Tuesday night’s play-in tournament game but added that he was disoriented after getting hit in the head.

video:video.video:description: King Charles III will make his first official state visit as monarch to the United States in late April, visiting Washington, D.C., New York and Virginia.

video:video.video:description: When a Ukrainian agricultural tycoon founded a volunteer unit of 30 people in the early days of Russia’s invasion, he had no certainty he would live to see what came next — but he did, and so did the force he created.

video:video.video:description: Eight months after the collapse of an alleged $140 million Ponzi scheme, Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger announced a third of the victims will obtain a $6.7 million settlement.

video:video.video:description: More than 300 trucks left the headquarters of Peru’s National Office of Electoral Processes early Saturday in southern Lima, carrying election materials for Sunday’s general election in Peru.

video:video.video:description: For decades, billions of gallons of sewage and industrial waste have been dumped into the Tijuana River.

video:video.video:title: AP finds ICE officers working before background checks, despite prior issues
video:video.video:description: Some new ICE officers began work before passing background checks and had past problems. In January, ICE said it added 12,000 officers to double its force for Trump’s deportation campaign.

video:video.video:description: An attorney for a man shot by federal ICE agents during an arrest in central California says his client didn't try to run over officers with his car and disputed claims he has a warrant out for his arrest in El Salvador.

video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 3rd: One crew member rescued after US fighter jet is shot down in Iran; Israeli airstrikes kill nearly two-dozen in Lebanon; US employers added 178,000 jobs last month; Artemis II's astronauts capture Earth's blue beauty in photos

video:video.video:title: Cheers erupt during Artemis II re-entry in San Diego watch party
video:video.video:description: Families and space fans attending a watch party at the San Diego Air & Space Museum cheer on as the first astronauts to visit the moon in more than a half-century splashdown in the Pacific.

video:video.video:title: Harlem Globetrotters teach Pope Leo to spin basketball on his finger
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV welcomed the Harlem Globetrotters to St. Peter’s Square on Wednesday. He was seen attempting one of the team’s classic tricks — spinning their red, white, and blue basketball on a finger — with help from one of the players

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 3-9 April 2026.

video:video.video:title: Marilyn Monroe collection reveals personal life
video:video.video:description: Items from the Marilyn Monroe Icon Collection were on display at a press preview at the Waldorf Astoria New York recently, including personal documents that reveal her private life and one of the last items she signed.

video:video.video:title: Former housemates speak about man charged in Atlanta-area killings
video:video.video:description: Two women who once lived in the same house as a man charged in a string of deadly attacks in the Atlanta suburbs speak out about his behavior prior to his arrest (AP Video: Ron Harris)

video:video.video:title: UN chief says international law is ‘being trampled’ especially in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres is urging a resumption of U.S.-Iran negotiations, a continuation of the ceasefire and the respect for freedom of navigation, including in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Israel Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field
video:video.video:description: Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field

video:video.video:title: Gaza woman scrapes living making and selling dolls to bring some joy to kids in war-ravaged territory
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are using arts and crafts to express their trauma from the war and earn an income, as hopes of returning to normal life remain limited with the territory’s humanitarian needs being overshadowed by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Pope doubles down on message of peace and unity as Trump's criticism continues
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday doubled down as U.S. President Donald Trump’s criticism showed no sign of letting up, insisting that the message "the world needs to hear today” is one of peace and dialogue.

video:video.video:title: Expectations for talks between Lebanon and Israel as Beirut mourns more than 300 dead
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese capital of Beirut was still reeling Friday from the shock of Israeli attacks two days prior, the deadliest day in the country since the renewed war between Israel and Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah erupted in March.

video:video.video:title: Tribal gas stations offer a reprieve from high prices during Iran war
video:video.video:description: Junelle Lewis was on the hunt for a reprieve from Seattle-area gas prices driven high by the Iran war when an app on her phone gave her the answer: the Tulalip Reservation north of the city, almost half an hour from her home.

video:video.video:title: Rain across northern Michigan heightens concerns of river flooding, dam overflows
video:video.video:description: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer has declared a state of emergency at the Cheboygan Lock and Dam Complex as record snowfall in March and the recent rain have elevated water levels.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife convicted of attempted manslaughter
video:video.video:description: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife convicted of attempted manslaughter

video:video.video:title: Leaders vote in Bulgaria's national elections
video:video.video:description: The election could bring to power a left-leaning ex-president just days after Hungarian voters rejected the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement of Viktor Orbán in Hungary.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian Prime Minister Orbán is ejected after 16 years in a European electoral earthquake
video:video.video:description: Hungarian voters have ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions.

video:video.video:title: Israeli authorities recover bodies from building hit by Iranian missile
video:video.video:description: Israeli rescuers on Monday removed bodies of people from the rubble of a building hit by an Iranian missile in Haifa on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Crowds revel at cherry blossoms in Tokyo as they near full bloom
video:video.video:description: Cherry blossoms blanket parks in Tokyo as they near full bloom, drawing large crowds of tourists and local residents to revel at the pastel pink flowers.

video:video.video:title: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors
video:video.video:description: Jonathan, believed to be 193, is still kicking — albeit slowly — on the island of St. Helena. “It was a hoax,” Anne Dillon, head of communications on the island, told The Associated Press on Thursday about his alleged passing.
video:video.video:tag: St. Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha

video:video.video:title: 'Euphoria' cast reunites at LA premiere
video:video.video:description: Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney stun at the launch of the new season of 'Euphoria' in Hollywood.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un meets Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi
video:video.video:description: Kim Jong Un voiced support for China’s push to build a “multipolar world” and called for deeper ties between the traditional allies during a meeting with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi, state media said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 15
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 15th: U.S. and Iran trade barbs as clock ticks over ceasefire; Pope Leo doubles down on message of peace and unity; severe storms cause damage in Michigan; stinky flower back in bloom in Massachusetts.

video:video.video:title: Polling stations open in Peru to elect a president and a bicameral Congress
video:video.video:description: Polling stations in Peru opened their doors on Sunday for the general election, in which Peruvians will choose their next president from an unusually large field of 35 candidates, hoping that the winner will be able to lead the fight against rising crime.

video:video.video:title: BookCon returns after six-year hiatus
video:video.video:description: After a six-year hiatus, BookCon 2026 is back to New York and bringing literature lovers together. (April 19)

video:video.video:title: Descendants say the theft of body parts from Pennsylvania grave sites is not a victimless crime
video:video.video:description: Descendants whose relatives’ burial sites were disturbed in a crime spree involving the theft of more than 100 bodies from Pennsylvania cemeteries speak outside the court after the defendant waived his right to an evidentiary hearing.

video:video.video:title: 'Euphoria' lead cast reunites for season three premiere
video:video.video:description: Zendaya, Jacob Elordi, Sydney Sweeney, Maude Apatow, Hunter Schafer and Alexa Demie reunited on the red carpet for the season three premiere of "Euphoria." (Apr. 7)

video:video.video:title: US Navy seizes an Iranian-flagged cargo ship
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. forcibly seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to get around a naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, the first such interception since the blockade of Iranian ports began last week.

video:video.video:title: Officials: Stabbings on New York subway leaves 3 hurt as officers shoot and kill knife-wielding man
video:video.video:description: A man with a machete who attacked three people randomly at a major New York City subway station on Saturday morning was shot and killed by police, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: County prosecutor charges ICE agent with assault for pointing gun at people on Minneapolis highway
video:video.video:description: The prosecutor believes it is the first criminal case brought against a federal immigration officer involved in the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown that surged federal authorities into cities across the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Suicide bomber attack kills at least five at police station in northwestern Pakistan
video:video.video:description: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a police station in the Bannu district of northwestern Pakistan late Thursday, killing at least five people and wounding several others, local police chief Rafi Ullah said.

video:video.video:title: UK PM 'absolutely furious' over latest developments in vetting of Mandelson for US role
video:video.video:description: Following the revelation in the Guardian newspaper, the UK government said Prime Minister Keir Starmer was not aware the Foreign Office had overruled a security vetting process for Peter Mandelson to become UK ambassador to Washington “until earlier this week.”

video:video.video:title: Haitians cut back on already scarce food and ask how they'll survive rising fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Haiti, the most impoverished country in the Western Hemisphere, has been hit the hardest by rising oil prices that experts warn will deepen a spiraling humanitarian crisis.

video:video.video:title: Fire damages Australian oil refinery, further reducing nation’s fuel supply threatened by Iran war
video:video.video:description: A fire has broken out at an Australian oil refinery, further reducing the nation’s fuel supply already threatened by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: US taxpayers help Cyprus upgrade military installations for humanitarian aid
video:video.video:description: Cyprus' main Evangelos Florakis naval base, just 142 miles (229 kilometers) from Lebanon’s coast, will get a new, U.S.

video:video.video:title: Dan Levy is an action star (and he loves it)
video:video.video:description: Dan Levy wanted to be an action star, so he wrote new TV series 'Big Mistakes'

video:video.video:title: Why Cameron Diaz doesn't Google herself
video:video.video:description: "Outcome" star Cameron Diaz reflects on life in the spotlight and why she doesn't Google herself.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits Catholic shrine in Angola that was a center of African slave trade
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited Sunday a Catholic shrine in Muxima, Angola, a former epicenter of the African slave trade, as he arrived in the southern African country a day earlier with a message of encouragement for its long-suffering people.

video:video.video:title: FIFA World Cup tickets near $11,000 as prices surge
video:video.video:description: FIFA World Cup final ticket prices are climbing to nearly $11,000 — far higher than previous tournaments. Here’s what’s behind the surge.

video:video.video:title: Iran says talks are 'continuation of defence and a continuation of war'
video:video.video:description: Negotiations have begun between the United States and Iran in Pakistan, days after a fragile, two-week ceasefire was announced.

video:video.video:title: Macron and Starmer hold international summit on reopening the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The leaders of France and the U.K. will gather countries from Europe and beyond – but not the United States – on Friday to push forward plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil route choked off by the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Video brings new scrutiny to Minneapolis ICE shooting after charges collapse
video:video.video:description: The city of Minneapolis released a video showing a chase and scuffle that ended in a nonfatal shooting in January and the suspensions of two federal officers involved in the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown in Minnesota.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana mass shooting victims mourned at vigil
video:video.video:description: Mourners gathered in a parking lot to honor the eight children killed in a Louisiana mass shooting. (AP Video/Sophie Bates)

video:video.video:title: US ready to hit Iran with economic pain equivalent to bombings, top Trump official says
video:video.video:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warns that the US is preparing to ramp up economic pain on Iran by levying secondary sanctions on financial institutions that do business with Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Bridge in Iran struck by US not used by military, local officials say
video:video.video:description: Iran state media reported that a US strike on the B1 bridge killed at least eight people and wounded 95.

video:video.video:title: Singer D4vd is arrested on suspicion of killing of a 14-year-old girl found dead in his Tesla
video:video.video:description: Singer D4vd has been arrested on suspicion of killing a 14-year-old girl whose decomposed body was found last year in his apparently abandoned Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills, authorities said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Hope and skepticism: the mixed feelings on the two sides of the border as Lebanon and Israel hold talks
video:video.video:description: Hope and skepticism: mixed feelings on the two sides of the border as Lebanon and Israel hold talks

video:video.video:title: Chernobyl's radioactive landscape testament to nature’s resilience
video:video.video:description: Four decades on, Chernobyl remains too dangerous for humans. But the wildlife has moved back in. Wolves now prowl the vast no-man’s-land spanning Ukraine and Belarus, and brown bears have returned after more than a century.

video:video.video:title: Long Island architect Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to murdering 7 women
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who led a secret life as a serial killer pleads guilty to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes

video:video.video:title: Philippine President Marcos debunks health rumors with jumping jacks
video:video.video:description: An annoyed Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. did a few rounds of jumping jack exercise and jogged briefly outside his office in front of journalists Monday to disprove rumors of his failing health.

video:video.video:title: UN warns about Mexico's plight of disappearances
video:video.video:description: Mexico faces a prolonged crisis of disappearances that has pushed families to become investigators, searching for traces of loved ones in clandestine burial sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II engine firing was 'flawless,' NASA says
video:video.video:description: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts fired their engines and blazed toward the moon Thursday night, breaking free of Earth orbit for the first time since 1972.

video:video.video:title: US troops complete their withdrawal from an air base in Syria
video:video.video:description: U.S. forces completed their withdrawal from Qasrak Air Base in Syria's Hasakah province Thursday evening, with the final convoy of soldiers and equipment leaving the site.

video:video.video:title: Inside an LA Justin Bieber look-alike contest
video:video.video:description: Fans gathered in West Hollywood for a Justin Bieber look-alike contest during Coachella weekend, and AP was there. Take a look inside.

video:video.video:title: James McAvoy comes clean about lying for an audition
video:video.video:description: James McAvoy makes his directorial debut with "California Schemin'" - a true story about a rap duo from Dundee who pretended to be American while seeking success - something that McAvoy can relate to after trying on various accents to secure film roles.

video:video.video:title: Video shows Tiger Woods being arrested after Florida crash
video:video.video:description: Newly released video shows Tiger Woods hiccuping and appearing to nod off in the back of a patrol car after crashing his vehicle last week in Florida.

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents picnic in parks on last day of Nowruz despite airstrikes on country
video:video.video:description: The annual public picnic day, called Sizdeh Bedar, which comes from the Farsi words for “thirteen” and “day out,” is a legacy from Iran’s pre-Islamic past that hard-liners in the Islamic Republic never managed to erase from calendars.

video:video.video:title: Why Cameron Diaz doesn't Google herself
video:video.video:description: Cameron Diaz stars in new dark comedy "Outcome" - a film about fame and cancel culture. In a recent interview with The Associated Press, she reflects on her own experience of living life in the spotlight.

video:video.video:title: Dave Chappelle stands firm through backlash and considers revisiting ‘Chappelle’s Show’
video:video.video:description: Dave Chappelle finds clarity away from the spotlight in the Ohio village where he lives. In an interview, he reflects on his comedic voice before the opening of a restored 19th-century schoolhouse that now houses a public radio station.

video:video.video:title: Israel's main airport resumes full operations as Iran ceasefire holds despite friction
video:video.video:description: Israel’s main airport resumed full operations on Thursday after the Iran war stranded tens of thousands of people, including both Israelis abroad and visiting tourists.

video:video.video:title: Eyes are on the Bab el-Mandeb Strait after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday made expletive-filled threats against Iran and its infrastructure if it doesn't open the Strait of Hormuz by his Tuesday deadline, after American forces rescued a wounded aviator whose Iran-downed plane fell behind enemy lines.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to a 10-day ceasefire starting Thursday
video:video.video:description: Israel and Lebanon opened negotiations this week in Washington aimed at forging a peace agreement. President Donald Trump announced that he will invite both leaders to meet at the White House.

video:video.video:title: Displaced relieved at Lebanon ceasefire but shocked by extent of destruction as they return home
video:video.video:description: A 10-day truce appeared to be holding in Lebanon early Friday, promising a pause in fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and possibly clearing one major obstacle to a deal between Iran and the United States and Israel to end weeks of devastating war.

video:video.video:title: China company offers glimpse into how AI could help reduce impact of synthetic textile waste
video:video.video:description: In an industrial park in Zhangjiagang, a small city on China's east coast, a large humming and hissing machine feeds on piles of used clothes and sorts them.

video:video.video:description: Hungarian voters have ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions.

video:video.video:title: Election loss for Hungarian Prime Minister Orbán has ripple effects for Trump, US conservatives
video:video.video:description: In a stunning blow to Viktor Orban, Hungarian voters have ousted the prime minister after 16 years in power, which could have significant reverberations in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Iran war energy shocks drive nuclear power plans in hard-hit Asia and Africa
video:video.video:description: The energy shock from the Iran war is boosting nuclear power generation among some nations and adding momentum to the atomic desires of hard-hit countries in Africa and Asia.

video:video.video:title: Opposition leader Peter Magyar votes in election that could unseat Orbán after 16 years
video:video.video:description: Hungarian prime-minister Viktor Orbán's top challenger, Péter Magyar voted on Sunday, as Hungarians were casting ballots in what is widely seen as Europe's most consequential election this year, a vote that could unseat populist Orbán, an ally of U.S.

video:video.video:title: As China develops renewable energy, its coal miners are facing the pressure of major change
video:video.video:description: As China prioritizes renewable energy over coal, miners are being forced to confront uncertainty. (AP video shot by: Wayne Zhang)

video:video.video:title: American gas prices remain high amid ongoing Strait of Hormuz closure
video:video.video:description: The Iran war has strained global fuel supplies, as the Strait of Hormuz remains a vital choke point for international trade.

video:video.video:title: Powerful winds and reported tornadoes rip through the Midwest, leaving heavy damage but no deaths
video:video.video:description: A trail of damaged homes and buildings dotted a wide swath of the U.S. on Saturday after a burst of destructive winds and reported tornadoes tore off roofs, uprooted trees and rendered rural roads impassable with debris.

video:video.video:title: Animals killed after Russia strike damages Kyiv region veterinary clinic
video:video.video:description: Animals were killed after a Russian strike damaged a veterinary clinic in the Kyiv region on Friday, the State Emergency Service reported.

video:video.video:title: BeWell: Some young men are discovering the wellbeing benefits of crocheting
video:video.video:description: In Italy some young men are embracing crafts traditionally associated with women, such as crochet, discovering the wellbeing benefits of mindful crafting.

video:video.video:title: Two killed in drone attack on civilian house in Irbil, authorities say
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed after an explosive-laden drone launched from Iran struck a civilian home in Irbil province, Iraq early Tuesday, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II crew share emotional moment over loved one lost after breaking record
video:video.video:description: Moments after breaking Apollo 13’s record, the astronauts asked permission to name two fresh lunar craters already observed.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13’s distance record with daring moon flyby that included a solar eclipse
video:video.video:description: After traveling deeper into space than any other humans, the Artemis II astronauts pointed their moon ship toward home Monday night, wrapping up a lunar cruise that revealed views of the far side never beheld by eyes until now.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians queue for hours in pouring rain to get bread, amid food shortage in war torn Gaza
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are struggling to meet their daily bread needs amid shortages of supplies entering the enclave and a lack of money.

video:video.video:title: A look at life on board an Iraqi commercial oil tanker amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: On board an oil tanker navigating the tense waters between the Gulf of Oman and the Strait of Hormuz, Iraqi Captain Rahman Al-Jubouri continues to work in one of the world’s most volatile maritime corridors, where the U.S.

video:video.video:title: New species of spider captured on video for the first time
video:video.video:description: A new species of spider has been captured on video for the first time. Researchers in southern Spain are racing to learn more about the tiny Cryptodrassus michaeli arachnid and the ecosystem it inhabits.

video:video.video:title: Chester Zoo welcomes the birth of one of world's tiniest antelopes
video:video.video:description: Zookeepers at Chester Zoo said Dotty will reach just 40cm in height when fully grown. The fawn will be raised by a surrogate father after her biological father died of old age shortly before her birth.

video:video.video:title: Miniature art transforms an alley into a surrealist wonderland at "Mississippi's Tiniest Museum"
video:video.video:description: The Hattiesburg Pocket Museum, which started as a way to spread joy during the covid-19 pandemic, has since drawn more than 300,000 visitors eager to explore the alley's often colorful, sometimes hidden displays.

video:video.video:title: Major Colorado pileup involving about 70 vehicles sends 8 to the hospital
video:video.video:description: Officials say crashes involving about 70 vehicles have bottlenecked a section of a snowy Colorado highway and sent eight people to the hospital.

video:video.video:title: Why wildfires are burning longer than they used to
video:video.video:description: Wildfires used to die down and even stop at night with cooler temperatures and lower humidity. But a study says climate change is making burning weather more around the clock in North America because night is becoming warmer and drier.

video:video.video:title: House extends surveillance powers until April 30 after late-night revolt sinks GOP plan
video:video.video:description: The House has approved a short-term renewal until April 30 of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies.

video:video.video:title: Iraq's oil hub slows to crawl as Strait of Hormuz shutdown strangles exports
video:video.video:description: Iraqi oil fields once alive with the buzz of workers are nearly deserted. Ports that pulsed with the churn of cargo have fallen still, the din of commerce replaced by the soft rhythm of waves.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers lose hope for humpback whale stranded in Baltic Sea
video:video.video:description: Rescuers said Wednesday that they have lost hope for the humpback whale that has become stranded repeatedly off Germany's Baltic Sea coast and now expect it to die in the inlet where it currently lies.

video:video.video:title: Pope in Cameroon's separatist west says 'world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants'
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV blasted those he claimed were "ravaging" Earth with war and exploitation, as he preached a message of peace Thursday in the epicenter of a separatist conflict considered one of the world’s most neglected crises.

video:video.video:title: Fresh starters attend underwater welcoming ceremony in western Japan
video:video.video:description: First of April marks the start of a new fiscal and academic year in Japan, a time widely regarded as a fresh start.

video:video.video:title: Iranian minister dismisses Trump threat in AP interview
video:video.video:description: Iran’s culture minister on Sunday dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump’s latest threats, calling him an “unstable, delusional figure. (AP video shot by Bassam Hatoum)

video:video.video:title: Israeli ambassador says Israel and Lebanon are ‘on the same side of the equation’ after DC talks
video:video.video:description: Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Yechiel Leiter said Tuesday the first negotiation talks between Israel and Lebanon were "positive", saying about the two countries "we are on the same side of the equation."

video:video.video:title: Iran's UN envoy says Tehran will not 'stand idle' if Trump follows through on threats
video:video.video:description: Iran's representative to the U.N. said Tehran would “take immediate and proportionate” action if President Donald Trump follows through on his threats to attack the country’s “whole civilization” if Iran does not make a deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts have 'extraordinary human experience' with daring moon flyby
video:video.video:description: After traveling deeper into space than any other humans, the Artemis II astronauts pointed their moonship toward home Monday night, wrapping up a lunar cruise that revealed views of the far side never beheld by eyes until now.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Eric Swalwell of California says he will resign from Congress after sexual assault allegations
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell says he will resign from Congress as a number of sexual assault allegations have come to light.

video:video.video:title: Finland's plan to bury spent nuclear waste not without risk to future generations
video:video.video:description: After decades of construction, the world’s first facility for permanently disposing spent nuclear fuel is set to begin operations in Finland, becoming a final resting place for tons of dangerous radioactive waste.

video:video.video:title: New video shows divers recovering Artemis II astronauts after splashdown
video:video.video:description: New video when U.S. Navy divers reach and open the Orion capsule of Artemis II reaching the astronauts for the first time in 10 days after their trip to the moon.

video:video.video:title: Column of smoke rises skywards in Tehran following latest Israel-US strike
video:video.video:description: Explosions were heard and a column of smoke was seen rising in Tehran early on Wednesday following the latest Israel-U.S. strike on the Iranian capital.

video:video.video:title: Mounted New York police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in Manhattan
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: What you need to know about the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The narrow, bending waterway in the Persian Gulf is a key trade route between the Middle East and the rest of the world, with global impact.

video:video.video:title: Stay tuned Cincinnati: WKRP is coming to town for real, North Carolina station's director says
video:video.video:description: In a case of life imitating art, there could soon be a real "WKRP in Cincinnati." A North Carolina nonprofit that has used the famous call letters for a low-power radio broadcast has signed a deal to let a group launch a station in the Ohio city that was home to the fictional station from the popula

video:video.video:title: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts return home to cheers after a record-breaking trip
video:video.video:description: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts are back home and feted to a thunderous welcome. Still marveling over their record-breaking lunar fly-around, the crew of four flew to Houston's Ellington Field from San Diego on Saturday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: Nature puts heat on blast as scorching temperatures take aim at eastern US
video:video.video:description: A long-lasting weather pattern is blasting hot air across the eastern United States. The heat wave threatens to shatter temperatures records Wednesday in cities like New York, Philadelphia, and Washington, D.C.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he views war as 'very close to over'
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he thinks the war in Iran is 'very close to over.' Trump commented in an interview with Maria Bartiromo of Fox Business News.

video:video.video:title: Happy 69th birthday to Fatou, the world's oldest gorilla living in captivity
video:video.video:description: The world’s oldest gorilla living in captivity has celebrated her 69th birthday. Fatou spent Monday munching on cherry tomatoes, beets, leeks and lettuce at the Berlin Zoo (AP video shot by Fanny Brodersen).

video:video.video:title: Digital detox: welcome to the offline club for those wanting a screen-free connection
video:video.video:description: A new kind of club has emerged in Amsterdam - one where participants choose to go offline, away from their phones and other devices. (AP video by Aleksandar Furtula)

video:video.video:title: Britain celebrates late Queen Elizabeth II as a style icon to mark her centenary
video:video.video:description: Britain celebrates late Queen Elizabeth II as a style icon to mark her centenary

video:video.video:title: Search and rescue efforts to find survivors after Israeli strikes kill at least 182 in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The attacks in Lebanon came hours after a ceasefire was announced in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Trump: Strait blockade is in effect but Iran wants a deal 'very badly'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump confirmed Monday a U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports is underway, saying “we can’t let a country blackmail” the world.

video:video.video:title: You aren't the only one who just sits in the car before or after a long day
video:video.video:description: Pausing to decompress in a parked car can help you reset, if you keep a few things in mind. That temporary pause can act as a buffer between one part of the day and the next.

video:video.video:title: ICT building at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology reduced to rubble after more US-Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: An airstrike hit Monday an information and communication technology building at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology, a professor there said.

video:video.video:title: Dizzying fuel prices are determined by factors largely outside of a gas station's control
video:video.video:description: The near-daily changes in U.S. gas prices have been dizzying for drivers. Experts say differences in price aren’t typically decided by any individual gas retailer, and most of them aren't pocketing the extra pennies when prices rise.

video:video.video:title: Brian Cox reveals his feelings about turning 80
video:video.video:description: Brian Cox says he's never been aware of his age — until he realized he was turning 80 on June 1. The "Succession" star's directorial debut is "Glenrothan."

video:video.video:title: Utility bills are exceeding mortgages in West Virginia despite Trump’s promised cuts

video:video.video:description: The small village of Hollókő in northeastern Hungary still observers an ancient Easter tradition to this day, when local men douse women with buckets of water during the holiday.

video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, April 18th: Iran fully closes Strait of Hormuz over US blockade; Mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves six dead; Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics; Powerful winds and reported tornadoes rip through the Midwest.

video:video.video:title: Humanoid robot chases wild boars in the Polish capital Warsaw
video:video.video:description: A viral video shows a humanoid robot chasing wild boars in the Polish capital Warsaw. The hero of the video published Sunday is Edward Warchocki, a humanoid robot turned Polish influencer.

video:video.video:title: Hands-on with Artemis II crew's luxury space watch
video:video.video:description: A watch built for space is back in the spotlight in Geneva. Omega’s Speedmaster line has been worn by astronauts for decades and is again drawing attention after being seen on the wrists of the crew of Artemis II.

video:video.video:title: Daniil Medvedev smashes racket seven times in 6-0, 6-0 defeat
video:video.video:description: Russia's Daniil Medvedev had a racket smashing moment in his 6-0, 6-0 defeat at Monte-Carlo Masters on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits Bemenda in Cameroon with message of peace, welcomed by jubilant crowds
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV preached a message of peace Thursday in the epicenter of a separatist conflict considered one of the world’s most neglected crises.

video:video.video:title: Polls close in Benin with finance minister favoured to succeed Talon as president
video:video.video:description: Polls closed in Benin on Sunday after voting to choose a successor to President Patrice Talon, who is stepping down after a decade in power, leaving a mixed legacy of economic growth, a growing jihadi insurgency in the north, and the suppression of opposition critics.

video:video.video:title: Russian strike damages port and energy infrastructure in Odesa
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian authorities said a drone attack overnight Thursday into Friday hit Odesa's port and energy infrastructure, damaging facilities and causing power disruptions.

video:video.video:title: A fast-growing wildfire in windy Southern California triggers evacuations
video:video.video:description: A smoky and fast-growing wildfire in Southern California has prompted multiple evacuation orders and warnings.

video:video.video:title: People in Moscow prepare for warm spring expected to break historical temperature records
video:video.video:description: The arrival of spring has brought unusually high temperatures to Russia's capital, to the delight of its residents.

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen's 'greatest delight'
video:video.video:description: Ian McKellen - who stars in new dark comedy "The Christophers" - looks back on his career.

video:video.video:title: Brazil's Indigenous leaders rally as land disputes and mining pressures grow
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Indigenous people marched in Brazil’s capital on Tuesday to protest what they say are violations of their land rights by large corporations advancing farming, logging and mining projects.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV carries cross for full Good Friday procession, the first pontiff to do so in decades
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV carried a wooden cross for all of the 14 stations of the Way of the Cross at the Colosseum on his first Good Friday as pontiff, marking the first time in decades that a pope carried the cross to every station.

video:video.video:title: Italy PM Meloni describes Trump's comments on Pope Leo XIV as 'unacceptable'
video:video.video:description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Tuesday described U.S. President Donald Trump's comments towards Pope Leo XIV as "unacceptable".

video:video.video:title: Researchers in Mexico develop easy-to-use robotic arm
video:video.video:description: Researchers in Mexico have developed a robotic arm they say reduces the complexity that often leads patients to abandon prosthetic devices.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13's record
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II astronauts are more than halfway to the moon. They are the first moonbound crew in more than half a century, picking up where NASA's Apollo program left off.

video:video.video:title: Pope calls for 'structures of solidarity' to help marginalised as he wraps up Cameroon visit
video:video.video:description: Friday marked the half-way point in Leo’s 11-day tour of four African nations. He leaves Saturday for Angola, another country blessed with oil and other natural resources, but where a third of the population lives on less than $2.15 a day.

video:video.video:title: Effigies burnt as part of the "Burning of Judas" ritual as Mexico marks Easter Saturday
video:video.video:description: Effigies of Donald Trump and other political figures were set alight Saturday evening as part of a "Burning of Judas" celebration in Mexico City.

video:video.video:title: French soldier killed in attack on peacekeepers in Lebanon on second day of ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The attack near the southern Lebanese village of Ghandouriyeh came after a 10-day ceasefire went into effect at midnight Thursday between Israel and Lebanon's militant Hezbollah group.

video:video.video:title: Two Pennsylvania firefighters in UTV killed in head-on crash during search for missing woman
video:video.video:description: Two firefighters in a utility vehicle have died in a head-on crash in Pennsylvania. The accident happened Saturday around 6 p.m. when their utility vehicle collided with a Toyota Camry on Route 222.

video:video.video:title: "It's very exciting:' Trump says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a ceasefire
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a temporary ceasefire, calling it "very exciting" and saying talks could take place at the White House soon.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth asks the Army's top uniformed officer to step down while US wages war against Iran
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has asked the Army’s top uniformed officer, Gen. Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Drone attack hits fuel warehouse in northern Iraq linked to BP oil and natural gas giant
video:video.video:description: Flames and black smoke billowed from the motor oil storage site in the Kurdish region of Iraq following Wednesday's attack.

video:video.video:title: Christian faithful gather in Gaza City to mark Good Friday
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Christian faithful gathered in the Holy Family Church to mark Good Friday in Gaza City, celebrating in silence and reverence.

video:video.video:title: White House defends Trump's 'moral high ground' after threat to Iran's 'whole civilization'
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt defended President Donald Trump’s warning that a "whole civilization will die," insisting he retains the "moral high ground" against Iran and calling the remark a "very strong threat" that delivered results.

video:video.video:title: President Trump and first lady host King and Queen of the Netherlands, PM Jetten at White House
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump welcomed King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands to the White House on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Bruno Mars, the 'New king of Las Vegas' is celebrated with Strip parade
video:video.video:description: Bruno Mars, the 'New king of Las Vegas' is celebrated with Strip parade

video:video.video:title: What you need to know as US and Iran say Strait of Hormuz fully open to commercial vessels
video:video.video:description: Iran said Friday it fully reopened the Strait of Hormuz to commercial vessels, but President Donald Trump said the American blockade on Iranian ships and ports “will remain in full force” until Tehran reaches a deal with the U.S., including on its nuclear program.

video:video.video:title: $13 train fare spikes to $150 for World Cup fans attending matches in New Jersey
video:video.video:description: New Jersey Transit officials announced Friday that fans trying to get to MetLife Stadium from New York City can expect to shell out $150 for a round-trip train fare for each match.

video:video.video:title: Rising fuel costs present 'huge issue' to German freight company
video:video.video:description: Freight companies are suffering as fuel prices in Germany rose to record highs, with the nationwide average price for diesel Tuesday exceeding 2.50 euros ($2.89).

video:video.video:title: Three-year-old killed among 5 others in two separate Israeli attacks
video:video.video:description: Israeli attacks killed five Palestinians, including a three year old and a 15-year-old in two separate incidents in northern Gaza and Gaza City Tuesday, according to a health official at Shifa hospital, where the bodies arrived.

video:video.video:title: 'I thought I was dead': Survivor of Israeli strike on Beirut recalls from hospital
video:video.video:description: A day after intense Israeli strikes pounded Lebanon’s capital, survivors on Thursday recounted scenes of carnage as hospitals struggled to cope with a surge of casualties.

video:video.video:title: Wombats go on display in France in zoo's new Australian section
video:video.video:description: The Beauval zoo, in Saint-Aignan, has unveiled two of the furry marsupials this April. It's the only place to see them in the country. (AP Video by Alexander Turnbull)

video:video.video:title: Colombia's environment minister says Middle East crisis should speed energy transition
video:video.video:description: Ahead of talks among some 50 countries about moving away from polluting fuels like oil and gas, Colombia's environment minister is arguing that the war in Iran underscores the need for a fast transition to clean energies like solar, wind, and geothermal.

video:video.video:title: One Tech Tip: A new generation is reviving the iPod for distraction-free listening
video:video.video:description: Remember the iPod? It's making a quiet comeback. Four years after Apple killed off its digital music player, secondhand sales are surging, It's fueled in part by young people interested not just in its retro looks but a desire to listen to music in a focused way and with playlists not determined by

video:video.video:title: Hunt on for robbers who held 25 people hostage at bank in Italy
video:video.video:description: Robbers broke into a bank in Naples on Thursday, holding 25 people hostage before escaping through the sewers, having drilled a hole in the floor during the night, according to the Italian news agency LaPresse.

video:video.video:title: Pam Bondi is out at US Attorney General
video:video.video:description: Pam Bondi is out as US Attorney General, ending the tenure of a loyalist who upended the Justice Department’s culture of independence from the White House.

video:video.video:title: Markets in Asia shows "disappointment" in Trump's speech on Iran military operation, analyst says
video:video.video:description: Tokyo’s Nikkei 225 was down 1.4% to 53,004.81 in early Asia trading, while South Korea’s Kospi lost 3.4% to 5,292.36 and Hong Kong’s Hang Seng fell 0.8% to 25,082.59.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV blasts ‘delusion of omnipotence’ fuelling US-Israeli war in Iran
video:video.video:description: In his strongest words yet, Pope Leo XIV on Saturday denounced the “delusion of omnipotence” that is fuelling the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Apollo astronauts brought these items to the moon's surface
video:video.video:description: While Artemis II astronauts make their way around the moon and back, take a look at some Apollo items that have touched the lunar surface.

video:video.video:title: Exclusive Mandarin Oriental Miami hotel vanishes in seconds with implosion
video:video.video:description: According to Swire Properties, the demolition will make way for the groundbreaking of The Residences at Mandarin Oriental, Miami.

video:video.video:title: Reaction from Tel Aviv after Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Residents in Tel Aviv are expressing hope and skepticism on the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Peru's presidential candidates criticize polling station issues in Lima affecting thousands of voters
video:video.video:description: Peruvian voters will have to wait until at least Monday to learn the outcome of Sunday’s presidential election after the process was mired with logistical issues that left thousands of people in the country and abroad unable to cast ballots.

video:video.video:title: Another US strike on suspected drug boat in the eastern Pacific kills 4
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched a strike on another boat accused of carrying drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing four people in the fourth such attack announced in the past few days.

video:video.video:title: US condemns Russia and China for veto on UN resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: U.S. ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz assailed the countries that objected to a U.N. Security Council resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: People react after Israeli Prime Minister approved direct talks with Lebanon
video:video.video:description: People react after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that he has authorized direct negotiations with Lebanon aimed at disarming Iranian-backed Hezbollah militants and establishing relations between the two countries.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran war 'going swimmingly' despite rising costs, casualties and uncertainty
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he was feeling positive about the Iran war despite energy price spikes, death and destruction, and anxiety over NATO and the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: 3 state troopers plead not guilty to charges connected to death of recruit after boxing match
video:video.video:description: A supervisor and two instructors with a Massachusetts State Police tactical unit plead not guilty in connection with the death of a recruit who suffered a concussion during a sparring session and blunt force injuries a day later in what investigators called an “unapproved and unsafe” boxing match.

video:video.video:title: Preparations as Trump says US negotiators will be in Pakistan on Monday for talks with Iran
video:video.video:description: Iran did not immediately confirm the talks but its chief negotiator, parliament speaker Mohammed Bagher Qalibaf, said in an interview aired on state television late Saturday that “there will be no retreat in the field of diplomacy,” while acknowledging a wide gap remained between the sides.

video:video.video:title: Iranian delegation leaves Islamabad after US-Iran ceasefire talks end without agreement
video:video.video:description: Iranian delegation leaves Islamabad after US-Iran ceasefire talks end without agreement

video:video.video:title: Harlem Globetrotters team members teach Pope Leo to spin basketball on finger
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV enjoyed a lighthearted moment Wednesday as he welcomed the U.S. exhibition basketball team the Harlem Globetrotters to St. Peter’s Square.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, April 16th: Trump announces Israel-Lebanon ceasefire; Europe could run out of jet fuel in "weeks," IEA says; Trump's arch design concept approved; Paramount Skydance CEO promises to extend movies theatrical window.

video:video.video:title: Trump plans to attend Supreme Court hearing on his bid to limit birthright citizenship
video:video.video:description: The citizenship restrictions are a part of Trump’s broader immigration crackdown, signed as an executive order in January 2025, but they have not yet taken effect anywhere in the country after being blocked by several courts.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: 'Wow!' The eye surgery marathon that restored sight for some South Africans
video:video.video:description: An 84-year-old South African woman is among more than 130 patients who had their sight restored during a free cataract surgery “marathon” aimed at tackling long backlogs in the public health system.

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage shows Tiger Woods being arrested at scene of car crash
video:video.video:description: Bodycam video shows Tiger Woods being placed in hand cuffs on suspicion of driving under the influence after his vehicle crashed in Florida on March 27th.

video:video.video:title: Without fuel, Cubans use their well-known creativity to get around the city
video:video.video:description: As the fuel crisis in Cuba continues, the island's residents are switching up how they travel. (AP shot by Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán

video:video.video:title: How biometrics are revolutionising EU travel
video:video.video:description: A new digital border system set to replace passport stamping across much of Europe will be fully implemented from 10 April 2026.

video:video.video:title: Designer Easter treats in stunning colours and shapes
video:video.video:description: A chocolatier in Barcelona has created high-end traditional Catalan treats for Easter. Known as 'monas', they can come in a variety of shapes and flavours.

video:video.video:title: Escaped wolf captured and returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search
video:video.video:description: Escaped wolf captured and returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search

video:video.video:title: Artemis crew saw things they 'had never even imagined'
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II mission crew held a news conference from on board the Orion spacecraft on Wednesday, as Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Jeremy Hansen said he saw things he "had never even imagined."

video:video.video:title: Mounted police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in New York
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts speak with President Trump after moon flyby
video:video.video:description: The four astronauts on board the Artemis II spoke with President Donald Trump on after their record-breaking trip around the moon.

video:video.video:title: Why Jonah Hill chose Keanu Reeves to be canceled in 'Outcome'
video:video.video:description: Director Jonah Hill reveals why he cast Keanu Reeves in his new dark comedy, "Outcome," as an actor struggling to keep his head above water bwhen being blackmailed over a compromising video.

video:video.video:title: Clouded leopard cub is already making adorable squeaks at the Nashville Zoo
video:video.video:description: Nashville Zoo is celebrating a conservation milestone following the birth of a female clouded leopard cub, the 50th born at the zoo since 1991.

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage shows Tiger Woods being arrested
video:video.video:description: Bodycam video shows Tiger Woods being placed in hand cuffs on suspicion of driving under the influence after his vehicle crashed in Florida on March 27th.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, April 5: Trump threatens Iran over the Strait of Hormuz.; Israeli‑Lebanese border tensions high; Artemis II aims to go farther from Earth than Apollo 13; Pope Leo XIV calls for peace during his first Easter Mass.

video:video.video:title: CCTV footage appears to show kidnapping of US journalist Shelly Kittleson in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Shelly Kittleson, a freelance journalist who has worked for years in Iraq and Syria, remains missing after her abduction on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Woman escapes from police car while handcuffed
video:video.video:description: A woman who shimmied out the rear window of a police cruiser while handcuffed and then fled over the weekend was arrested Tuesday, authorities in western Michigan said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 8
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 8th: US, Israel and Iran agree to tentative ceasefire; Israeli strikes central Beirut, killing scores ; Rex Heuermann pleads guilty in the Gilgo Beach killings; Charles and Camilla celebrate 21st wedding anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Beirut neighborhood hit overnight in fatal Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed in an Israeli strike early Wednesday in Beirut's Jnah neighborhood.

video:video.video:title: Preserving rural Jewish life is the focus of a Maine center this Passover
video:video.video:description: The Center for Small Town Jewish Life at Colby College is growing as Passover ends this year. The center began a decade ago with a goal of supporting Jewish congregations that are far from big cities.

video:video.video:title: Former Migos rapper Offset shot outside a Florida casino
video:video.video:description: Offset, a former member of the influential hip-hop trio Migos, was shot outside a Florida casino and is in stable condition, a spokesperson said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics, including the controversial drug ibogaine
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Saturday directed his administration to speed up reviews of certain psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, which recently has been embraced by combat veterans and conservative lawmakers despite having serious safety risks.

video:video.video:title: One killed and more than a dozen injured in train crash at level crossing in Northern France
video:video.video:description: A high-speed train driver was killed on Tuesday in northern France when his locomotive collided into a truck at a level crossing in Northern France.

video:video.video:title: Tanker truck in Texas erupts into fiery blaze after collision
video:video.video:description: A tanker truck in Texas hauling gasoline erupted into a fiery blaze on Sunday morning after colliding with another vehicle and knocking over power lines, leaving the truck driver in critical condition.

video:video.video:title: Stampede at Haitian mountaintop fortress leaves at least 25 dead and dozens injured
video:video.video:description: A stampede at a mountaintop fortress popular with tourists in northern Haiti has left at least 25 people dead and several dozen others injured.

video:video.video:title: Why 'unretired' seniors are picking up gig work to pay the bills
video:video.video:description: A growing number of Americans have “unretired” in recent years. After ending careers that spanned decades, they returned to the workforce due to rising living costs, insufficient retirement savings, and a desire to stay active.

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem believes music can unify
video:video.video:description: 2026 Eurovision contestant Delta Goodrem responds to the boycott of the song contest by five countries who did not want Israel to take part. (April 10)

video:video.video:title: JetBlue raises its checked bag fees as the Iran war affects fuel costs
video:video.video:description: U.S. carrier JetBlue has raised its checked bag fees by as much as $9 as the war in the Middle East disrupts global oil supplies and increases fuel prices.

video:video.video:title: Iranian-American says his family in Tehran wants the Iranian regime gone
video:video.video:description: Iranian-American Ali Khaleghi talked to his family in Tehran two weeks ago and said they were anxious, not because of the war going on in their country, but because the Iranian regime is still there.

video:video.video:title: Sky turns eerie red as storm blows Sahara sand dust into atmosphere in Greece
video:video.video:description: The skies over the Greek island of Crete took on Martian hues on Wednesday. The eerie spectacle was the result of strong winds blowing stifling dust across the Mediterranean from North Africa.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya attends 'Euphoria' premiere in LA
video:video.video:description: Zendaya strutted down the carpet on Hollywood Boulevard to celebrate "Euphoria's" season three premiere in Los Angeles, Tuesday. (April 7)

video:video.video:title: James Marsden shakes things up in 'Your Friends & Neighbors'
video:video.video:description: James Marsden plays new character Owen Ashe - a millionaire with an extravagant lifestyle and many secrets - who disrupts the wealthy community of Westmont Village in season 2 of Apple TV's "Your Friends & Neighbors."

video:video.video:title: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors
video:video.video:description: Jonathan the tortoise, believed to be 193, is still kicking - albeit slowly - on the island of St. Helena despite rumors of his death.
video:video.video:tag: St. Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha

video:video.video:title: The oldest juke joint in Mississippi keeping the blues alive since 1948
video:video.video:description: The oldest juke joint in Mississippi, and perhaps the whole of the U.S., is still keeping the blues alive 78 years after first opening its doors.

video:video.video:title: London basks in hot sunshine - for now
video:video.video:description: London basks in hot sunshine - for now

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 17th: U.S. and Iran say Strait of Hormuz now open to commercial shipping; Thousands return home amid ceasefire in Lebanon; Accused Charlie Kirk shooter seeks ban on cameras in courtroom; Optimism over Iran war sends stocks soaring.

video:video.video:title: Record low Colorado mountain snow threatens grim year for dry US West
video:video.video:description: Hydrologists working high in the Rocky Mountains have measured what they say is Colorado’s driest winter of snow moisture on record.

video:video.video:title: After crash, Tiger Woods told deputy he was 'talking to the president,' body camera shows
video:video.video:description: After crashing his SUV last week in Florida, Tiger Woods took out his phone and told a deputy, “I was just talking to the president,” according to body camera footage released Thursday showing Woods' arrest on a DUI charge.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian election winner Péter Magyar says his government ‘will be a constructive partner' in EU
video:video.video:description: Hungarian election winner Péter Magyar promised a beginning of a new era during an international news conference after his Tisza party's victory in Budapest on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Another teen runs for governor in Vermont, this time making it to the general election ballot
video:video.video:description: Looking back, gubernatorial candidate Dean Roy says his political ambitions started in the eighth grade. And by that he means, last year.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 12
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, April 12: President Donald Trump says U.S. will begin a blockade on Strait of Hormuz; Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam postpones trip to Washington; Hungarians cast their ballots in test for PM Victor Orbán; Russia and Ukraine have accused each other of violating a 32-hour Ortho

video:video.video:title: Strikes on alleged drug boats kill 5, leave 1 survivor in eastern Pacific, US military says
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military says it blew up two boats accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Man indicted in connection with 2 'Texas Killing Fields' cases
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors say a man they believe is connected to some of the “Texas Killing Fields” deaths of dozens of women found near Houston since the 1970s has been indicted.

video:video.video:title: Rotting eggs, sun-baked diapers and more: corpse flower captivates visitors
video:video.video:description: Crowds gathered at Mount Holyoke College’s Talcott Greenhouse to witness the rare bloom of “Pangy,” a corpse flower known for its powerful odor resembling decaying flesh.

video:video.video:title: Haitians say they are suffering as soaring oil prices deepen food crisis
video:video.video:description: A displaced woman in Haiti describes how extreme poverty and rising oil prices have forced her family to survive on just one meal a day amid a worsening food crisis.

video:video.video:title: House approves aviation safety bill based on deadly midair collision near Washington
video:video.video:description: An aviation safety bill seeking to address lessons learned from last year’s midair collision of a jet with an Army helicopter near Washington, DCl was approved by the House Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Police block protesters marching toward Venezuela presidential palace
video:video.video:description: A group of protesters attempting to march to Venezuela's Presidential Palace in Caracas were stopped by police on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: A native seabird population is surging in Honolulu, finding a home among office towers and traffic
video:video.video:description: Manu-o-Kū, a seabird native to Hawaii, is flourishing in the middle of Honolulu's concrete towers, traffic-clogged roads and Waikiki hotels, defying the fate of many other native birds in Hawaii.

video:video.video:title: Iranian American recalls fear after Trump threat to destroy Iran’s civilization
video:video.video:description: Hossein Baninajar, an Iranian American who has lived in the U.S. for 11 years, said he feared losing his mother and sister if President Donald Trump had followed through on a threat to destroy Iran’s civilization Tuesday night.

video:video.video:title: Streets of Beirut filled with displaced people as Israel continues military operation
video:video.video:description: It's been a month since Hezbollah fired rockets into Israel after the U.S.-Israeli attack on its patron, Iran, triggering Israeli bombardment of Lebanon and a ground invasion.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's interior ministry investigating shooting in Kyiv
video:video.video:description: A gunman who killed at least six people in the streets of Kyiv Saturday before taking hostages and barricading himself in a nearby supermarket has been shot dead by police, Ukrainian officials said.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans celebrates Easter Sunday with a rainy French Quarter Parade
video:video.video:description: Parade-goers traveled to the historic streets of the French Quarter in New Orleans for an annual Easter Sunday parade in the rain. (AP Video: Stephen Smith)

video:video.video:title: Robots crack jokes and grasp items at Humanoid Robot Expo in Tokyo
video:video.video:description: They can dance and they're good at doing repetitive work we humans tire of, but the big question at the Tokyo Expo is when will the humanoids be able to look after us at home.

video:video.video:title: How Pakistan has emerged as a mediator between US and Iran
video:video.video:description: It was initially seen as an unexpected mediator, but this week Pakistan has established itself as a key player in bringing Iran and the United States to the negotiating table.

video:video.video:title: Young voters in Hungary played crucial role in ending Orbán's 16-year rule
video:video.video:description: On Sunday, young voters celebrated the victory of pro-European candidate Péter Magyar. Polls showed 65% of voters under 30 supported Magyar before the vote.

video:video.video:title: Myanmar's ruling general elected as President, keeping army in charge
video:video.video:description: Myanmar’s parliament on Friday elected Min Aung Hlaing, a general who ousted Aung San Suu Kyi’s civilian government in 2021 and kept an iron grip on power for the past five years, as the country’s new president.

video:video.video:title: Massive warehouse fire in southern California; one person arrested
video:video.video:description: An arson suspect was arrested after a massive fire erupted at a warehouse in Ontario; more than 100 firefighters were on the scene battling the blaze

video:video.video:title: Louisiana GOP races to scrap elected office won by a man exonerated after 30 years in prison
video:video.video:description: Calvin Duncan has been elected as New Orleans' new clerk of criminal court, but now Republican lawmakers in Baton Rouge are racing to eliminate the position.

video:video.video:title: Remains of three Indonesian United Nations peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon arrive in Jakarta
video:video.video:description: The remains of three Indonesian United Nations peacekeepers who were killed while on escort duty supporting U.N. operations in southern Lebanon arrived in Jakarta on Saturday evening, where President Prabowo Subianto led a solemn welcome ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Pope says Trump's threat to destroy Iranian civilization is 'truly unacceptable'
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said Tuesday that U.S. President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy Iranian civilization was “truly unacceptable” and said any attacks on civilian infrastructure violate international law.

video:video.video:title: Trump's threats to destroy Iran power plants raise war crime questions
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump threatened to blow up every bridge and power plant in Iran, action that would be so far-reaching that some experts in military law said it could constitute a war crime.

video:video.video:title: Colombia approves plan to cull roaming hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
video:video.video:description: Colombian officials on Monday authorized a plan to cull dozens of hippos roaming freely through a region in the center of the country, where they threaten villagers and displace native species years after notorious drug lord Pablo Escobar brought in the first ones.

video:video.video:title: Rohingya refugee who survived boat capsizing describes being rescued, losing others
video:video.video:description: At least 250 people, including Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals, were either feared dead or missing after a boat capsized in the Andaman Sea recently on the way to Malaysia, according to the U.N. refugee and migration agencies.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV pushes back against President Donald Trump’s comments in feud over Iran war

video:video.video:description: Patriarch Theophilos III washed the feet of Orthodox priests in Jerusalem on Thursday in a ceremony made more special by the reopening of the Holy Sepulchre.

video:video.video:description: Dozens of firefighters were battling a large blaze at Rio De Janeiro's Olympic Park Velodrome on Wednesday.

video:video.video:description: The first astronauts to visit the moon in more than a half-century are back on Earth. Artemis II's crew of four have emerged one-by-one from their lunar capsule Friday after a splashdown in the Pacific.

video:video.video:description: Officials say a gunman in Louisiana killed eight children, including seven of his own, and shot two other people Sunday in an early morning attack of domestic violence carried out across two houses.

video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who led a secret life as a serial killer pleaded guilty on Wednesday to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

video:video.video:description: International observers arrived in Lima ahead of the country's presidential election on Sunday. Voting is mandatory for Peruvians from the ages of 18 to 70.

video:video.video:description: An Israeli drone killed a Palestinian man Monday in the Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said. The strike hit a bike in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood in Gaza City and also wounded a child, according to the Shifa hospital which received the casualties.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump admits to posting an image of himself as a healer, seeming to make comparisons between himself and Jesus Christ.

video:video.video:description: Speaking to reporters outside the White House on Saturday, U.S. President Donald Trump claimed military victory against Iran and downplayed the importance of ongoing ceasefire negotiations involving Vice President JD Vance because “regardless what happens we win.”

video:video.video:description: Republican Clay Fuller has won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia. Fuller’s special election win Tuesday bolsters a slim GOP majority.

video:video.video:description: A new rare earth mineral facility that uses what it claims to be a ground-breaking extraction technique is set to open in South Africa, with US investment.

video:video.video:description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer described the two-week ceasefire as "highly fragile" and stressed that Lebanon should be included in it.

video:video.video:description: As Earth warms due to human-caused climate change, the ground isn’t staying frozen as consistently in many places, causing more frequent runoff events and in turn, more winter days when nitrates are likely to reach unhealthy levels.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said he had “no fear of the Trump administration" when he responded to a reporter's question on board the papal plane en route to Algiers on Monday.

video:video.video:description: Hydrologists working high in the Rocky Mountains have measured what they say was Colorado’s worst snowpack on record—an alarming fact for a state that supplies drinking water to millions across the drought-stricken American West.

video:video.video:description: Bluebell flowers were in full bloom on Tuesday in the Belgian city of Halle. Tourists and photographers visited Hallerbos forest to experience the annual bloom.

video:video.video:description: The U.S. did not respond immediately to requests for comment on the claim. It was not clear what may have happened to the plane, including whether Iran was claiming it was shot down or had another issue.

video:video.video:description: During Holy Week and particularly on Good Friday, tens of thousands of people make the pilgrimage to the northern New Mexico village of Chimayó.

video:video.video:title: Peru election stretches into a second day after ballot delivery failures

video:video.video:title: Iranian Jews commemorate slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Members of Iran's Jewish community commemorated Iran's late supreme leader on Thursday. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in an airstrike on his residence on Feb. 28, the day the United States and Israel started a war against Iran.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's top military advisor, Gen. Dan Kaine, describes Navy warships maintaining the blockade against Iran as "like driving a sports car through a supermarket parking lot on a payday weekend."

video:video.video:description: The hilly Christian town of Ain Saadeh east of Beirut was in shock Monday after an Israeli airstrike crashed into an apartment building there, killing an anti-Hezbollah politician, his wife and another woman.

video:video.video:title: Sweat, plunge, repeat: UK embraces the sauna boom
video:video.video:description: Scandinavians have long-embraced the ritual of the sauna, but now people in the UK are signing up for the hot experience - with a floating pod on the River Thames.

video:video.video:title: Peru election stretches into a second day after ballot delivery failures
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Peruvians were back at the polls on Monday for a second day of voting after failure to deliver ballots to voting centers extended Sunday’s election by a day.

video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasted the US media as “unpatriotic” Thursday, slamming its coverage of the Iran war as "garbage" and likening the press to the biblical Pharisees who came into conflict with Jesus.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV walked Tuesday in the footsteps of his spiritual father, St. Augustine, making a pilgrimage to the archaeological ruins in Algeria where the fifth-century titan of early Christianity lived, died and wrote some of the most important works in Western thought.

video:video.video:description: A group of critically endangered mountain bongos bred in European zoos are being prepared for transfer back to Kenya, as part of a long term effort to rebuild wild populations of one of Africa’s rarest large mammals.

video:video.video:description: Oil prices are plunging back toward $95 per barrel, and stock markets are surging worldwide after President Donald Trump pulled back from his threat to force a whole civilization to die in the war with Iran.

video:video.video:description: In Peacock's "The Miniature Wife," Elizabeth Banks plays a woman shrunk by her husband, played by Matthew Macfadyen. (April 17)

video:video.video:description: A likely tornado tore through a small village in northwest Illinois on Friday, ripping down power lines and roofs, uprooting trees.

video:video.video:description: As Ben & Jerry’s celebrated its annual Free Cone Day, co-founder Ben Cohen was focused on a different sort of freedom, calling on Ben & Jerry’s owner The Magnum Ice Cream Co. to sell the brand.
