video:video.video:description: Thierry Frémaux - the artistic director of the Cannes Film Festival - has said not to expect red carpet premieres like “Top Gun: Maverick” or “Mission: Impossible — The Final Reckoning” - both of which made splashy premieres in recent years - at the 2026 edition.

video:video.video:title: Oregon Zoo welcomes two penguin chicks
video:video.video:description: The Oregon Zoo welcomed two Humboldt penguin chicks that hatched about a week apart in March. Both are healthy and will nest with their parents until they are 3 months old.

video:video.video:description: Martha Kelly details her first day working on the “Euphoria” set with Zendaya. Kelly plays Laurie, a drug lord who terrorizes Zendaya’s character in the hit HBO series.

video:video.video:description: Bangladesh is conducting emergency measles-rubella vaccinations while trying to contain an ongoing outbreak that has killed more than 100 children in less than a month.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo hits back at Trump over Iran war
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV pushed back against President Donald Trump's criticism of his stance on the Iran war. He said that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he didn’t fear the Trump administration.

video:video.video:description: One of the astronauts, Christina Koch, said there was an issue with the toilet but she acted as the plumber when the time came.

video:video.video:description: Britney Spears has checked herself into substance abuse treatment facility just over a month after she was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs.

video:video.video:description: NASA is preparing for its first crewed mission towards the moon in more than fifty years. The Artemis II mission will not land on the lunar surface, but it's a critical step in testing deep space travel and the crew reflects a new generation of international and more diverse human spaceflight.

video:video.video:description: In Steven Soderbergh’s “The Christophers,” stars Ian McKellen and Michaela Coel make one of the more memorably on-screen pairs in recent memory.

video:video.video:title: Don Cheadle, Ayo Edebiri set for inclusive Broadway debut in ‘Proof’
video:video.video:description: Don Cheadle and Ayo Edebiri reflect on the significance of their Broadway debuts in a revival of “Proof,” David Auburn’s Tony Award and Pulitzer Prize-winning play.

video:video.video:title: Elle Fanning had to explain Only Fans to Nicole Kidman for new show context
video:video.video:description: Elle Fanning had to explain Only Fans to Nicole Kidman for new show context

video:video.video:title: Iran marks 40-day mourning ceremony for slain supreme leader
video:video.video:description: In the capital Tehran, mourners wearing black began their rally from Jomhouri Eslami Square to the neighborhood of the office of Ali Khamenei in the heart of Tehran.

video:video.video:title: 'We have a lot of agreement:' Trump expresses optimism over a deal with Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump expressed renewed optimism Thursday over a potential deal with Iran, saying talks could resume as soon as this weekend and suggesting he may even travel to Pakistan to sign a peace agreement if one is reached.

video:video.video:title: United Nations dispatching special envoy to Middle East
video:video.video:description: United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres says the spiral of death and destruction in the Middle East must stop.

video:video.video:title: Spring lambs audition for starring role in London's sheep drive
video:video.video:description: Spring lambs at a farm in the UK are "auditioning" for a starring role in London. The newborn lambs could soon be crossing one of London's busy bridges across the river Thames as part of the annual London Sheep Drive, a tradition dating back to medieval times.

video:video.video:title: Pope calls for Angolans to took to the future
video:video.video:description: Leo celebrated Mass before an estimated 100,000 people outside the capital and again sought to encourage Angolans.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters tackles flames after Russia strikes port infrastructure in Odesa
video:video.video:description: Russia has struck the Black Sea port city of Odesa with drones overnight, hitting civilian and port infrastructure, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Severe storms leave damage behind in Kansas town
video:video.video:description: A day after severe storms damaged communities in the Plains and the Midwest, forecasters warned that storms could bring giant hail, tornadoes and severe wind gusts to the regions again on Tuesday afternoon and evening.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Offset shot and stable after incident at Seminole Hard Rock in Florida
video:video.video:description: The rapper Offset was shot in Florida, according to a spokesperson. The Seminole Police Department said a person sustained injuries that were not life-threatening Monday evening at a valet area at the Seminole Hard Rock in Hollywood, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Israel and Hezbollah clash in strategic Lebanese village ahead of official talks
video:video.video:description: Israel has stepped up its strikes and ground invasion in southern Lebanon, where it intends to create a security zone along the Litani River, almost 20 miles (32 kilometers) from the border.

video:video.video:title: Towering fountain of lava seen in Hawaii’s Kilauea Volcano
video:video.video:description: A tower of lava was visible, shooting up inside the Kilauea volcano on the Big Island of Hawaii on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Video shows gunfight between attackers and police outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul
video:video.video:description: Video shows gunfight between attackers and police outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul

video:video.video:title: Solar farm helps Kentucky sheep farmer profit, grow flock
video:video.video:description: Kentucky sheep farmer Daniel Bell has been expanding thanks to a partnership with a solar company that pays him to graze his flock among the panels. (AP Video: Joshua A.

video:video.video:title: Republican Clay Fuller wins election to U.S. House in Georgia’s 14th District
video:video.video:description: Republican Clay Fuller on Tuesday won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia, turning back a Democratic challenge with the help of President Donald Trump’s endorsement despite uneasiness over the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Drivers across the US are feeling the pinch as both new car and gas prices continue to climb
video:video.video:description: American motorists are feeling pain at the pump as well as the dealership (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Earthquake off Indonesia topples buildings
video:video.video:description: The undersea magnitude 7.4 earthquake toppled buildings in parts of northern Indonesia, sent people fleeing from their homes, killed at least one person and generated a small tsunami Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Vance heads overseas for negotiations aimed at ending Iran war
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance is warning Iran not to “play” the U.S. as he heads for negotiations aimed at ending the war.

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti hopes 'The Testaments' will inspire Gen-Z to feel empowered and 'enact change'
video:video.video:description: Chase Infiniti stars in the highly anticipated "Handmaid's Tale" spin-off "The Testaments" and hopes the new series will inspire Gen Z to create change in their own communities.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street swings sharply as uncertainty increases about what will happen in the war with Iran
video:video.video:description: The S&P 500 fell as much as 1.2% Tuesday, hours ahead of a deadline by President Donald Trump to destroy Iranian power plants and bridges.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry takes part in Indigenous smoking ceremony in Australia
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry, the Duke of Sussex, took part in an Indigenous smoking ceremony at the Australian War Memorial in Canberra.

video:video.video:title: Surging oil prices spark protest in Haiti as workers demand salary increase
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of factory workers marched for the third consecutive day in Port-au-Prince Wednesday, to demand a raise in the minimum salary amid the recent oil price hike.

video:video.video:title: Gaudí confirmed as architect of unique mountain refuge
video:video.video:description: A long-running debate over a mountain chalet in Catalonia may finally have been settled. Officials now say the unusual building was designed by none other than Antoni Gaudí.

video:video.video:title: Strikes hit Beirut as Israel vows to continue attacking Lebanon despite Iran ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israel's attacks came hours after a ceasefire was announced in the US-Israeli war with Iran. Israel has said the agreement does not extend to Lebanon, although mediator Pakistan said it does, and vowed to continue its ground invasion in the south.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong and other Asian markets retreat on concerns of fragile Middle East ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Oil rose again to above $97 a barrel and Asian stocks were trading lower Thursday on skepticism over a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Officials warn of crisis in Gaza City as residents struggle with rodent 'infestations'
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in Gaza City are reporting massive infestations of rodents, as life in the displacement tents has become increasingly brutal.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese military withdraws from border towns as Israeli military pushes inwards
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese military said its forces have largely withdrawn from some remaining border towns as Israeli troops continue to push through southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday April 6th: U.S. and Iran received draft proposal for ceasefire; More strikes in Iran and Israel; Wounded U.S. aviator rescued; Artemis II to swing around Moon on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather for festival celebrating Japanese culture in Ohio
video:video.video:description: An organization dedicated to bringing together Ohio’s Japanese and American communities hosted a festival Sunday with a stage performances and other activities related to Japanese culture.

video:video.video:title: How worms are helping to create healthy soil for Kenyan farmers
video:video.video:description: Worms are helping improve soil on Kenyan farms. A venture just outside the capital uses the creatures to eat agricultural waste and animal dung, turning it into nutrient-rich compost.

video:video.video:title: Two dead and 14 injured in fireworks warehouse explosion in Russia
video:video.video:description: An explosion at a fireworks warehouse in the southern Russian city of Vladikavkaz killed at least two people and injured 14 others, including a baby, local authorities said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Pope departs for Africa as Trump issues scathing Truth Social post
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is embarking Monday on the first-ever papal trip to Algeria, aiming to promote Christian-Muslim coexistence at a time of global conflict and honor the locally born inspiration of his religious spirituality, St.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 17
video:video.video:description: AP Top Stories April 17

video:video.video:title: Pro-Iran government demonstrators burn US, Israeli flags in Tehran following news of ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Pro-government demonstrators gathered and chanted in the streets of Iran’s capital Wednesday morning after the ceasefire was announced. (AP video shot by Mohsen Ganji)

video:video.video:title: Cherry blossom trees falling triggers safety concern as Japan marks the season
video:video.video:description: Many of Tokyo's popular and iconic Somei Yoshino cherry blossom trees were planted during Japan's postwar advancement in the 1960s, and are now getting old and frail.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV remembers his predecessor in Easter Monday blessing
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV remembered his predecessor Pope Francis’s "profound witness of faith and love" during the traditional Easter Monday blessing.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Pooh Shiesty charged with kidnapping over alleged dispute involving rapper Gucci Mane's label
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors in Texas accuse rapper Pooh Shiesty and eight others of robbing three men at gunpoint and kidnapping them following a contract dispute in January involving rapper Gucci Mane’s record label

video:video.video:title: South Africans react to ‘Lion King’ composer suing comedian
video:video.video:description: A Grammy-award winning South African composer who wrote and performed the iconic opening chant in “Circle of Life” for Disney's “The Lion King" movies is suing a comedian for allegedly damaging his reputation by intentionally misrepresenting the song's meaning.

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr on seeing Beatles bandmate Paul McCartney in concert
video:video.video:description: Ringo Starr reflects on watching Paul McCartney perform an intimate show at the Los Angeles Fonda Theatre in March.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s plan for a 250-foot Triumphal Arch in Washington gets approval
video:video.video:description: The concept for President Donald Trump’s 250-foot Triumphal Arch in Washington has been approved by a federal agency. Here's what to know.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drones hit industrial facilities and maternity ward in Russia
video:video.video:description: Russia’s Ministry of Defense said that its forces had destroyed 258 Ukrainian drones overnight on Saturday over 16 Russian regions, as well as over the annexed Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea and the Black and Azov seas.

video:video.video:title: Presidential candidates vote in election where 35 contenders are vying for ninth president in 10 years
video:video.video:description: Presidential candidates voted on Sunday in Lima as the country makes their pick in the general election for next president from an unusually large field of 35 candidates, amid concerns about crime, corruption, and the country’s persistent political instability.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian police shoot and kill a gunman who killed at least 6 and took hostages in a supermarket
video:video.video:description: A gunman who killed at least six people in Kyiv, Ukraine's capital, before taking hostages and barricading himself inside a supermarket on Saturday has been shot and killed by police, Ukrainian officials said.

video:video.video:title: Brian Cox is not ready to turn 80
video:video.video:description: "Glenrothan" director and star Brian Cox is going to turn 80 this summer but, aside from some "twinges here and there," he reveals he still feels as if he's nine-years-old.

video:video.video:title: Russia and China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz as US deadline for Iran deal looms
video:video.video:description: Russia and China on Tuesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz that had been repeatedly watered down in hopes those two countries would abstain.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians mourn Al Jazeera journalist Mohammad Wishah targeted by Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Palestinians gathered Thursday morning at Al-Aqsa hospital in central Gaza to bid a final farewell to Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah, who was killed in an Israeli drone strike the day before.

video:video.video:title: Nutella jar seen spinning through Artemis II
video:video.video:description: Minutes before the Artemis II mission shattered the distance record set by Apollo 13 in 1970 a jar of Nutella hazelnut spread was seen spinning through the spacecraft.

video:video.video:title: Michigan fans gather outside basketball arena in Ann Arbor to welcome the 2026 national champs home
video:video.video:description: Michigan fans gathered outside Crisler Center to welcome the freshly crowned 2026 men’s basketball national champions back to Ann Arbor (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Paris Fury's parenting advice for Molly Mae and Tommy Fury
video:video.video:description: Boxer Tyson Fury's wife Paris passes on family advice to brother-in-law Tommy Fury and his partner, reality TV star Molly-Mae Hague.

video:video.video:title: Funeral of 13 Lebanese service officers killed in Israeli strike on southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Thirteen members of Lebanon’s State Security Service were laid to rest on Saturday in Sidon, a day after being killed in an Israeli strike in the southern city of Nabatieh.

video:video.video:title: Fans react to opening night of BTS' world tour
video:video.video:description: Fans react to BTS’ return to the stage after nearly four years, at the opening night of the K-pop megastars' "ARIRANG" world tour.

video:video.video:title: Iran to take 'serious legal steps' to protect its cultural sites
video:video.video:description: Iranian Minister of Cultural Heritage, Tourism and Handicrafts Sayed Reza Salihi-Amiri said on Sunday that Iran would take "serious legal steps" to protect its cultural sites, after Israeli and U.S. bombardments have damaged over 100 sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: How are rising prices affecting people’s choices?
video:video.video:description: Rising prices are still hitting consumers, but PepsiCo says lowering prices helped bring shoppers back. Here’s how people are feeling about higher costs.

video:video.video:title: 'Ketamine Queen' who supplied drugs to Matthew Perry set for sentencing
video:video.video:description: A woman who sold actor Matthew Perry the drug ketamine that killed him is set to be sentenced Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Tension remain high at Israel-Lebanon border as Hezbollah attacks continue to set off sirens
video:video.video:description: Tensions remained high on the Israeli-Lebanese border as rocket and drone attacks by the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah continued to set off sirens throughout the day on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Baby camel born in UK zoo
video:video.video:description: Sophia was born to Bactrian camels mum Izzy and dad Oakley at Whipsnade Zoo in Bedfordshire, boosting the herd’s total to eight.

video:video.video:title: Find out what King Charles III will do on his state visit to America
video:video.video:description: King Charles III will make his first official state visit as monarch to the United States in late April, visiting Washington, D.C., New York and Virginia.

video:video.video:title: Measles threatens babies in South Carolina and the nation as community vaccination rates decline
video:video.video:description: Babies too young to be vaccinated are among the most vulnerable in measles outbreaks like the one in South Carolina, which is the largest the U.S. has seen in decades.

video:video.video:title: 1 killed, 2 presumed dead after partial roof collapse at parking garage being built in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a stairwell roof at a parking garage being built in Philadelphia collapsed suddenly, killing one person and leaving two missing and presumed dead.

video:video.video:title: Trump says it would take 4 hours to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump described the consequences that Iran would face if it didn’t reach a deal with the U.S. by Trump’s 8 p.m. Eastern Tuesday deadline.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy honored with prestigious International Four Freedoms Award for Ukraine's resilience
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was awarded the International Four Freedoms Award at a ceremony Thursday for his and his nation's courage and resilience in resisting the full-scale invasion launched more than four years ago by Russia.

video:video.video:title: Blind Massachusetts man is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets
video:video.video:description: Matthew Shifrin is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets using free accessibility instructions created by sighted and blind volunteers in his nonprofit group.

video:video.video:title: Pressure mounts on Kanye West to be pulled from headline role at summer music festival in London
video:video.video:description: Pressure was mounting Sunday on the American rapper Kanye West to be pulled from his headline role at a London music festival this summer.

video:video.video:title: Price hikes hit Greeks shopping in preparation for Orthodox Easter celebration
video:video.video:description: Price increases due to the US-Israel war on Iran have hit Greek shoppers as they prepare to celebrate Orthodox Easter on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Eyewitnesses recount three deadly Israeli strikes on medics in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: “We went to help. These aren't just civilians, they're our colleagues, so it was a little emotional for us,” Mohammed Jaber, 43, told The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Ex-Michigan coach Sherrone Moore gets probation for confrontation with woman who disclosed affair
video:video.video:description: Former Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore was placed on probation over a confrontation with his executive assistant soon after he was fired for having an inappropriate relationship with her (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Artemis II’s astronauts return to Earth with a splashdown in the Pacific
video:video.video:description: The three Americans and one Canadian have returned with a dramatic splashdown Friday evening, as their capsule parachuted into the Pacific to close out a nearly 10-day trip to the moon and back.

video:video.video:title: 'Malcolm in the Middle' cast returns for more chaos
video:video.video:description: Bryan Cranston, Frankie Muniz, and Jane Kaczmarek reunited Tuesday in New York to celebrate the new Hulu limited series “Malcolm in the Middle: Life’s Still Unfair."

video:video.video:title: Jon Hamm compares working on 'The Morning Show' with 'Your Friends and Neighbors'
video:video.video:description: Jon Hamm compares the “murderer’s row” of talent on "The Morning Show" to the Broadway-caliber lineup on "Your Friends and Neighbors." (April 15)
